seo.title: AI Agent Observability in AutoGen Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent observability in AutoGen agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AutoGen, AI agent observability challenges arise from multi-agent conversations where agents exchange messages via the ConversableAgent's chat_messages history and register_reply hooks, often leading to infinite loops or unsafe code execution. Common issues manifest as repeating message patterns in agent states, uncontrolled token accumulation in long-running group chats, and hallucinated agreements in debate scenarios. Vex integrates seamlessly as a reply function filter to monitor and intervene in real-time, providing deep visibility into agent behaviors.

howItManifests.symptom: Identical message pairs repeating >5 times in chat_messages history
howItManifests.impact: Infinite runtime, server crashes, token exhaustion

howItManifests.symptom: CodeExecutor agent returns exec() calls with rm/os.system() patterns
howItManifests.impact: Security vulnerabilities, data loss, system compromise

howItManifests.symptom: Multiple agents output identical wrong answers (e.g., 'Paris is capital of Germany')

howItManifests.scenario: Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates
howItManifests.symptom: GroupChat messages exceed 100 turns without termination_condition met

detection.name: Infinite Loop Detector
detection.description: Scans ConversableAgent chat_messages for repeating message patterns across agents

detection.name: Unsafe Code Detector
detection.description: Inspects code execution outputs for dangerous system calls using regex patterns

detection.name: Token Exhaustion Monitor
detection.description: Tracks message count and estimated token usage in group chat sessions

fixes.name: Loop Termination Reply Filter
fixes.description: Registers as reply function to break detected infinite loops

fixes.name: Safe Code Reply Override
fixes.description: Intercepts and sanitizes dangerous code before execution
fixes.code:
def safe_code_filter(sender, recipient):
    unsafe = detect_unsafe_code(recipient.last_message())
    if unsafe:
        return f'SAFETY_VIOLATION: {unsafe["pattern"]} detected. Using safe alternative.'
    return None

coder = autogen.AssistantAgent(name="coder", llm_config=...)
coder.register_reply(autogen.UserProxyAgent, safe_code_filter, position=0)

fixes.name: Token Budget Enforcer

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Observability Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex transforms AutoGen observability from manual debugging to automated reliability by registering as a reply function filter that intercepts every agent message before processing. Using the vex-sdk, you define observability rules as Python functions that analyze chat_messages, message content, and agent states in real-time, catching issues before they cascade across multi-agent systems.

The platform provides automatic remediation through configurable policies - from simple message termination to sophisticated human-in-the-loop escalation and model switching. Vex maintains full conversation lineage with token accounting, enabling cost governance across hundreds of concurrent AutoGen sessions. Integration takes <5 minutes via pip install autogen vex-sdk.
vexAutomation.code:
import autogen
import vex

# Install: pip install autogen vex-sdk

vex.init(project='autogen-observability')

@vex.reply_filter(name='autogen-loop-guard')
def autogen_loop_detector(sender, recipient):
    '''Detects infinite loops in AutoGen conversations'''
    loop = detect_infinite_loop(recipient)
    if loop:
        vex.incident('INFINITE_LOOP', loop, agents=[sender.name, recipient.name])
        return '🔒 Loop detected - conversation terminated.'
    return None

# Apply to all agents
config_list = [{'model': 'gpt-4o-mini', 'api_key': os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY')}]

user_proxy = autogen.UserProxyAgent('user')
assistant = autogen.AssistantAgent('assistant', llm_config={'config_list': config_list})

# Vex auto-registers across all conversations
user_proxy.initiate_chat(assistant, message='Solve this math problem...', reply_filter=vex.reply_filter)
